items.hook: Cecilia Fire Thunder is a nurse, community health planner and tribal leader of the Oglala Sioux.
items.body: On November 2, 2004, she was the first woman elected as president of the Tribe. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.body: Set in the 17th century and inspired by true events, the Tuscan filmmaker’s new movie starring Pierfrancesco Favino and Silvio Orlando will be presented Out of Competition

items.body:

items.hook: DevOps is the integration and automation of software development and information technology operations.
items.body: DevOps encompasses the tasks necessary for software development and can lead to both shortening development time and improving the development life cycle. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: As of 2022, PIN operates in 33+ countries.
items.body: People in Need is a Czech nonprofit, non-governmental organisation based in Prague, Czech Republic. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Oscar-winning actress Brenda Fricker dies aged 81
items.body: The Irish actress won the best supporting Oscar for ’My Left Foot’ in 1990.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: Walter Bell Franklin was an English cricketer.
items.body: Franklin was a right-handed batsman who fielded as a wicket-keeper. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: A payphone is typically a coin-operated public telephone, often located in a telephone booth or in high-traffic public areas.
items.body: Prepayment is required by inserting coins or telephone tokens, swiping a credit or debit card, or using a telephone card. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Trese is a Filipino komik series written by Budjette Tan and illustrated by Kajo Baldisimo.
items.body: It tells the story of Alexandra Trese, a detective who deals with crimes of supernatural origin. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Glassriver and Network Movie team up on Áróra TV drama adaptation - Production / Funding - Iceland/Germany
items.body: The Icelandic-German production partnership is adapting Lilja Sigurðardóttir’s bestselling crime novel series into an international TV drama set across Iceland and London
